tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: The Creative works sublime success, furthering through perseverance.
Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God, the LORD is one.
The Lord our God, the Lord is one.
Say, He is Allah, the One.
The ALL is ONE, the ONE is ALL.
The Dao that can be told is not the eternal Dao.
In the beginning this was Brahman, one only, without a second.

critical_distinctions: ICH/DDJ/UPN: impersonal process; TOR/NTB/QUR: personal covenant; ETT: mental substance.

tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: Perseverance furthers.
And it shall come to pass, if thou shalt hearken diligently unto the voice of the LORD thy God...
Whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap.
So whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it, And whoever does an atom's weight of evil will see it.
Therefore, without being attached to the fruits of activities, one should act as a matter of duty...

critical_distinctions: All teach ethical causality; timing differs (immanent vs. eschatological).

theme_name: The Illusion of the Material World

tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: Know then that Nature is Maya, and that the great God is the Lord of Maya.
The named is the mother of myriad creatures.
And what is the worldly life except the enjoyment of delusion.
And the world passeth away, and the lust thereof...

critical_distinctions: UPN treats materiality as fundamentally illusory (Maya); Abrahamic texts treat it as real but transient and corruptible.

counter_evidence.text: Torah affirms the material creation as 'very good' (Gen 1:31).

theme_name: The Principle of Correspondence (As Above, So Below)

tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: As above, so below, as below, so above.
Everything that exists in the lower world has its counterpart in the upper world.
Thy will be done in earth, as it is in heaven.
Looking up, we contemplate the brilliant phenomena of the heavens; looking down, we examine the definite arrangements of the earth.

critical_distinctions: ETT/ZOH view this as mechanical occult law; NTB views this as a petition for divine alignment.

counter_evidence.text: QUR emphasizes strict separation and transcendence of the Creator over creation (Tanzih).

tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: The great man is he who does not lose his child's heart.
And the LORD God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.
Neither shall they say, Lo here! or, lo there! for, behold, the kingdom of God is within you.
So when I have proportioned him and breathed into him of My Spirit, then fall down to him in prostration.
Seek ye and know that thy soul is an immortal flame, born from the fire of the Halls of Amenti.
The living entities in this conditioned world are My eternal fragmental parts. Due to conditioned life, they are struggling very hard with the six senses.
The soul of man is the candle of God, by which He searches all the inward parts of the belly.
Know the masculine, keep to the feminine, and be the valley of the world. Being the valley of the world, eternal virtue will never leave you.
That which is the finest essence — this whole world has that as its soul. That is Reality. That is Atman. That art thou, Shvetaketu.

critical_distinctions: UPN/BG: Atman is literally identical to ultimate reality (Brahman); TOR/ZOH: divine breath is a gift that remains distinct from God; NTB: indwelling kingdom, not identity; QUR: the Ruh is breathed in but God remains wholly transcendent (no ontological union); ETT: immortal spark as cosmological substance; DDJ/ICH: Te as innate virtue aligned with Tao, not a personal soul.

counter_evidence.text: QUR strictly prohibits hulul (divine indwelling/incarnation), making the Ruh a delegated property, not a divine identity (Surah 42:11 — nothing is like unto Him).

tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: The Book of Changes contains the measure of heaven and earth; therefore it enables us to comprehend the tao of heaven and earth.
Ye shall walk in all the ways which the LORD your God hath commanded you, that ye may live, and that it may be well with you.
Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
Guide us to the straight path — the path of those upon whom You have bestowed favor, not of those who have evoked anger or of those who are astray.
I, Thoth, the Atlantean, give of my wisdom, give of my knowledge, give of my power. Freely I give to the children of men.
Whenever and wherever there is a decline in religious practice, O descendant of Bharata, and a predominant rise of irreligion — at that time I descend Myself.
Come and see: there is a path that leads above and a path that leads below, and a man is judged according to the path he chooses to walk.
Highest good is like water. Water gives life to the ten thousand things and does not strive. It flows in places men reject and so is like the Tao.
Now therefore the description of Brahman: Not this, not this. For there is no other and more appropriate description than this Not this.

critical_distinctions: DDJ/ICH: the Way is impersonal, pre-ethical, spontaneous (wu-wei); TOR: Halakha is a legal-ethical walk defined by commandment; NTB: the Way is exclusively personified in Christ; QUR: Al-Sirat is a petitioned, divinely maintained road with eschatological consequences; BG: Dharma is caste/role-specific and upheld by divine descent; ZOH: the path is mystical-moral with kabbalistic structure; UPN: the path is epistemic negation toward formless Brahman.

counter_evidence.text: NTB's exclusive claim ('no man cometh... but by me') structurally conflicts with all pluralist path frameworks (DDJ, BG, UPN), making this a convergence of metaphor rather than doctrine.

tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: The Receptive brings about sublime success, furthering through the perseverance of a mare. The superior man has somewhere to go.
Now the man Moses was very meek, above all the men which were upon the face of the earth.
I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the faith of the Son of God.
Who, when disaster strikes them, say, Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return.
Conquer the self and thou shalt be free. Conquer the ego and thou shalt be the master.
Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.
Whoever makes himself as nothing before his Master, the Holy One, blessed be He, raises him on high.
Empty yourself of everything. Let the mind rest at peace. The ten thousand things rise and fall while the Self watches their return.
As rivers flow into the ocean and in so doing lose name and form, even so the wise man, freed from name and form, reaches the divine Person who is greater than the great.

critical_distinctions: DDJ/ICH: ego dissolution via non-striving, no theological agent required; BG/UPN: dissolution of individual self into cosmic/divine Self (Atman = Brahman); NTB/ZOH: kenosis/bittul retains personal identity in surrender — the self yields but is not annihilated; QUR: Fana is a Sufi concept not universally accepted in Islam; the base text (2:156) implies submission rather than metaphysical dissolution; ETT: ego conquest as an act of will, more voluntarist than the others.

counter_evidence.text: Mainstream Sunni Islam rejects Fana as a valid doctrinal concept; the cited verse (2:156) does not itself teach ego annihilation but rather patient acceptance of loss.

tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: Return. Success. Going out and coming in without error. Friends come without blame. To and fro goes the way. On the seventh day comes return.
Therefore prophesy and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, O my people, I will open your graves, and cause you to come up out of your graves.
So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption; it is raised in incorruption. It is sown in dishonour; it is raised in glory.
I swear by the Day of Resurrection. And I swear by the reproaching soul. Does man think that We will not reassemble his bones? Yes. We are Able to restore even his fingertips.
Know ye, O man, that far in the future, aeons and aeons hence, shall the soul drink again of the fountain of wisdom.
As a person puts on new garments, giving up old ones, similarly, the soul accepts new material bodies, giving up the old and useless ones.

All souls are subject to reincarnation; and people do not know the ways of the Holy One, blessed be He. They do not know that they are brought before the tribunal both before they enter into this world and after they leave it.
The ten thousand things rise and fall without cease, creating, yet not possessing. Working, yet not taking credit. Work is done, then forgotten. Therefore it lasts forever.
According as one acts, according as one conducts himself, so does he become. The doer of good becomes good. The doer of evil becomes evil. One becomes virtuous by virtuous action, bad by bad action.

critical_distinctions: TOR/NTB/QUR: physical bodily resurrection is a singular eschatological event — not a cycle; BG/UPN/ZOH: reincarnation is a repeating cycle with the goal of liberation (Moksha/Olam HaBa); DDJ/ICH: cyclic return is cosmological and impersonal, not soteriological — the 'return' is of nature, not the soul; ETT: cyclic incarnation as cosmic memory accumulation.

counter_evidence.text: Abrahamic traditions unanimously reject reincarnation as heresy. Hebrews 9:27 states 'it is appointed for men to die once' — a direct structural contradiction of the karma/gilgul framework.

tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: The eight trigrams determine good and ill fortune, and from this come the great achievement. The images reveal the complete universe; the judgments reveal the complete world.
And on the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had made. And God blessed the seventh day.
And I saw in the right hand of him that sat on the throne a book written within and on the backside, sealed with seven seals.
He who created the seven heavens in layers. You do not see in the creation of the Most Merciful any inconsistency. So return your vision to the sky; do you see any breaks?
Seven are the lords of the planes of existence, each with a name and a power divine.
By human calculation, a thousand ages in one day of Brahma, and a thousand ages constitute one night. One who knows this is a knower of day and night.
The ten Sefirot of Nothingness: ten and not nine, ten and not eleven. Understand with wisdom and be wise with understanding.
The Tao begot one. One begot two. Two begot three. And three begot the ten thousand things.
AUM is the bow. The self is the arrow. Brahman is the target. It is to be hit by an undistracted man. Then, as the arrow becomes one with the target, he will become one with Brahman.

critical_distinctions: ICH/DDJ: numbers as cosmological generative sequences — not objects of worship; ZOH: Sefirot as divine emanation architecture — number as ontological structure; TOR/NTB/QUR: sacred number as divine signature in creation and prophecy; BG/UPN: cosmic timescales as framework for liberation cycles; ETT: numerical planes as literal metaphysical tiers. Confidence is 3 (not 5) because the surface parallel (sacred number) is strong but the underlying mechanisms are highly heterogeneous.

counter_evidence.text: QUR explicitly warns against divination and numerology (Surah 5:90), creating tension between structural seven-heaven cosmology and any system that treats number as a divinatory or esoteric key.
